name: the-girl-from-the-train
description: Irma Joubert's The Girl from the Train — a sweeping historical novel about a Polish girl rescued from Auschwitz and raised in South Africa, exploring survival, dual identity, displacement, and love across divides.
useCase: Surviving childhood trauma, dual cultural identity, displacement and rebuilding home, love across boundaries, the courage to remember.
knownRisks: The novel is a work of fiction set against historical events — some liberties are taken
The book deals with the Holocaust and WWII trauma — handle with sensitivity
The South African context includes apartheid-era dynamics
